title: Design with Thought
description: Essays, studies, projects and notes by Brian Hinton — accessibility lead at Superhuman (formerly Grammarly).

heroAside.writing: An essay on screen-reader announcements as copywriting.
heroAside.building: Prism — a perceptual contrast checker. Alpha with four teams.
heroAside.reading: The Craftsman, second pass. Design Justice, slowly.

notes.body: Watched a colleague navigate our app with VoiceOver this morning. They reached the compose button in 19 tab stops. Our target was 4. There is a month of work in that gap.
Rereading The Design of Everyday Things. Norman is still right about almost everything, and still wrong about the same few things. I think the wrong bits are the most useful.
Tiny win: we landed focus-visible across the product tonight. 214 components. One PR. Twelve months of groundwork.
Mentorship conversation with J — she asked how to know when to stop investigating and start shipping. I said: when the next investigation would only rearrange the doubt, not reduce it.

reading.title: The Craftsman
reading.author: Richard Sennett
reading.note: Second read. The chapter on the hand is doing most of the work.

reading.title: Design Justice
reading.author: Sasha Costanza-Chock
reading.note: Slow read — one chapter a week, with notes.
